expectations.text: Looks for relevant patterns elsewhere in this repository instead of inventing the design in isolation.
expectations.evidence: The response references feature-dev, handoff template, create-plan, create-tasks, and code-architect patterns in this repo.

expectations.text: Creates a concrete file-backed handoff artifact at a path matching the workflow's handoff contract.
The artifact content follows a reusable template shape and includes implementation slices, file-level map, and validation plan.
expectations.evidence: The artifact includes sections for Implementation Slices, File-by-File Implementation Map, and Validation Plan, along with summary, goals, findings, assumptions, and kickoff steps.

expectations.text: Provides artifact status in the response with the exact artifact path.
expectations.evidence: final_response.md has an Artifact Status section with the exact absolute path to the created handoff plan.

expectations.text: Includes rationale or trade-offs for the proposed design.
expectations.evidence: The response includes policy trade-offs such as enforcement overhead versus reliability and questions about standard-track strictness.

claims.claim: Stopping before implementation should still create or update handoff-plan.md with artifact status.
claims.evidence: The run follows this behavior and produces both file artifact and response-level artifact status.

uncertainties: Whether Standard track should enforce artifact creation even when implementation completes in same turn remains open.
